name: External Control of System or Configuration Setting
mapping_rationale: This CWE entry is at the Base level of abstraction, which is a preferred level of abstraction for mapping to the root causes of vulnerabilities.

name: Configuration
mapping_rationale: Categories are not weaknesses

name: Improper Input Validation
mapping_rationale: CWE-20 is commonly misused in low-information vulnerability reports when lower-level CWEs could be used instead, or when more details about the vulnerability are available [REF-1287]. It is not useful for trend analysis. It is also a level-1 Class (i.e., a child of a Pillar). Finally, it is often used when the root cause issue is related to how input is incorrectly transformed, instead of "validated" to be correct as-is.

name: Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory ('Path Traversal')
mapping_rationale: This CWE entry might have children that would be more appropriate.

name: External Control of File Name or Path
mapping_rationale: This CWE entry is at the Base level of abstraction, which is a preferred level of abstraction for mapping to the root causes of vulnerabilities.

name: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements in Output Used by a Downstream Component ('Injection')

mapping_rationale: This CWE entry is at the Base level of abstraction, which is a preferred level of abstraction for mapping to the root causes of vulnerabilities.

name: Exposure of Sensitive Information to an Unauthorized Actor
mapping_rationale: CWE-200 is commonly misused to represent the loss of confidentiality in a vulnerability, but confidentiality loss is a technical impact - not a root cause error. As of CWE 4.9, over 400 CWE entries can lead to a loss of confidentiality. Other options are often available. [REF-1287].

name: Password Aging with Long Expiration

name: Improper Privilege Management
mapping_rationale: CWE-269 is commonly misused. It can be conflated with "privilege escalation," which is a technical impact that is listed in many low-information vulnerability reports [REF-1287]. It is not useful for trend analysis.
